back_translation: Then Job replied:
'If only my grief could be weighed and my calamity laid with it on the scales!
For then it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; that is why my words have been rash.
For the arrows of the Almighty are in me; my spirit drinks their poison; the terrors of God are arrayed against me.
Does a wild donkey bray when it has grass, or an ox low over its fodder?
Is tasteless food eaten without salt? Is there any taste in the white of an egg?
My soul refuses to touch them; they are like loathsome food to me.
Oh that my request might be granted, and that God would give me what I hope for:
that God would be willing to crush me, to let loose His hand and cut me off!
This would still be my comfort, and I would rejoice amid unrelenting pain, that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
What strength do I have, that I should still hope? And what is my end, that I should be patient?
Is my strength the strength of stones? Is my flesh made of bronze?
Is there any help left within me, now that success is driven from me?
A despairing man should have the kindness of his friend, even if he forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
But my brothers are treacherous like a wadi, like streambeds that overflow and pass away,
darkened by ice, with melting snow covering them,
but in the dry season they vanish; in the heat they dry up from their place.
Caravans turn aside from their routes; they go up into the wasteland and perish.
The caravans of Tema look for water; the travelers of Sheba hope to find it.
They are ashamed because they had trusted; when they arrive, they are disappointed.
Now you have become like that to me; you see something terrifying and are afraid.
Did I ever say, 'Give me something,' or 'Offer a bribe for me from your wealth,'
or 'Deliver me from the enemy's hand,' or 'Redeem me from the grasp of the ruthless'?
Teach me, and I will be silent; show me how I have erred.
How painful are honest words! But what does your argument prove?
Do you intend to correct only my words, treating the speech of a despairing man as wind?
You would even cast lots over an orphan and bargain away your friend.
But now please look at me—would I lie to your face?
Reconsider, let there be no injustice; reconsider, for my righteousness still stands.
Is there injustice on my tongue? Can my mouth not discern malice?'
